metadata.description: Service: Pod Stable Access Entry Points. ClusterIP/NodePort/LoadBalancer/Ingress. ### Explanation: - **ClusterIP**: This is the default service type that exposes the service on a cluster-internal IP. It's not accessible from outside the cluster. ```python # Example of creating a ClusterIP service in Kubernetes using Python with kubernetes client from kubernetes import client, config def create_clusterip_service(): v1 = client.CoreV1Api() body = { "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "Service", "metadata": {"name": "example-clusterip"}, "spec": { "selector": {"app": "example"}, "ports": [{"port": 80, "targetPort": 9376}] } } v1.create_namespaced_service(namespace="default", body=body) ``` - **NodePort**: This service exposes the service on each Node's IP at a static port (the `nodePort` field). It can be accessed from outside the cluster. ```python # Example of creating a NodePort service in Kubernetes using Python with kubernetes client def create_nodeport_service(): v1 = client.CoreV1Api() body = { "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "Service", "metadata": {"name": "example-nodeport"}, "spec": { "type": "NodePort", "selector": {"app": "example"}, "ports": [{"port": 80, "targetPort": 9376}] } } v1.create_namespaced_service(namespace="default", body=body) ``` - **LoadBalancer**: This service type is used to expose the service externally. It works with cloud providers and load balancers to provide a public IP address. ```python # Example of creating a LoadBalancer service in Kubernetes using Python with kubernetes client def create_loadbalancer_service(): v1 = client.CoreV1Api() body = { "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "Service", "metadata": {"name": "example-loadbalancer"}, "spec": { "type": "LoadBalancer", "selector": {"app": "example"}, "ports": [{"port": 80, "targetPort": 9376}] } } v1.create_namespaced_service(namespace="default", body=body) ``` - **Ingress**: This is a resource that manages external access to the services in a cluster. It typically sits in front of one or more services and defines routing rules. ```python # Example of creating an Ingress resource in Kubernetes using Python with kubernetes client def create_ingress(): networking_v1 = client.NetworkingV1Api() body = { "apiVersion": " Networking.istio.io/v1alpha3", "kind": "Ingress", "metadata": {"name": "example-ingress"}, "spec": { "rules": [ {"host": "example.com", "http": {"paths": [{"path": "/", "backend": {"service": {"name": "example-service", "port": {"number": 80}}}]}}} ] } } networking_v1.create_namespaced_ingress(namespace="default", body=body) ``` These examples use the Kubernetes Python client to create services and an Ingress resource.
